hawkish_phrases: inflation remained elevated and above the Committee's longer-run goal of 2 percent
The Committee remains highly attentive to inflation risks
inflation was still well above the Committee's longer-run goal and that a risk remained that progress toward price stability would stall
an easing in financial conditions beyond what is appropriate could make it more difficult for the Committee to reach its inflation goal
circumstances might warrant keeping the target range at its current value for longer than they currently anticipated
dovish_phrases: Participants viewed the policy rate as likely at or near its peak for this tightening cycle
almost all participants indicated that...a lower target range for the federal funds rate would be appropriate by the end of 2024
Inflation had eased over the past year
downside risks to the economy that would be associated with an overly restrictive stance
the extent of any additional policy firming that may be appropriate
summary: The FOMC unanimously voted to hold the federal funds rate at 5.25-5.50 percent while signaling that the policy rate is likely at or near its peak, with almost all participants projecting cuts by end-2024, representing a clear dovish shift despite continued attention to inflation risks.
